output.summary: ARIA accessibility attributes (role='list', role='listitem', alt text attribute on ImageCard) are non-visual changes. Visual QA confirms no layout regressions were introduced. The block renders correctly across all 12 demo-container variations at all breakpoints (375, 768, 1024, 1440) and all browsers (Chromium, Firefox, WebKit). Overall composition matches the Figma design at 785:34519.
output.compositionNotes: Compared implementation first variation (1440w) against Figma Card #=2+ variation. Layout structure (ThemeHeading with eyebrow/heading/description/buttons in 2-column layout, 3-column masonry grid with text and image cards, rounded corners, staggered row heights) matches the Figma design. Color difference (purple accent vs Figma's cyan) is expected theme accent color variation.

breakpointsChecked.notes: 3-column masonry grid renders correctly. Cards have proper proportions, rounded corners, staggered heights. ThemeHeading renders in 2-column layout with eyebrow, heading, description, and buttons. All 12 variations visible and structurally correct.
Layout matches 1440w with slightly narrower container. Grid columns and card proportions scale correctly. No overflow or clipping issues.
Grid collapses to 2 columns as expected. Cards stack appropriately with masonry stagger pattern. ThemeHeading stacks to single column. All content visible and readable.
Grid collapses to 1 column as expected. Cards stack vertically. All text readable, images display correctly with proper object-fit. No horizontal overflow.

crossBrowserChecks.notes: Reference browser. All breakpoints render correctly.
Matches Chromium rendering at all breakpoints. No browser-specific visual differences.
Matches Chromium rendering at all breakpoints. No browser-specific visual differences.

roleList.details: role='list' found on grid container div in all 12 block instances on the test page.

roleListitem.details: role='listitem' detected on card wrappers via child selector. 54 total cards across 12 sections all have the attribute.

altTextAttribute.details: ImageCard.php includes alt text logic: extracts $alt from attributes, conditionally passes to wp_get_attachment_image. Images on test page have alt attributes present (empty string for decorative, as expected when no custom alt is provided).

ariaLabelledby.details: 11 of 12 sections have aria-labelledby referencing their heading. The one without (section index 4 'Minimal') appears to not have a heading attribute set at block level, which is expected behavior for theme_block_props.

layout.notes: 3-column masonry grid with staggered row heights matches Figma. 2-column and 4-column variations also render correctly. Card positioning follows the nth-child patterns for masonry stagger.

typography.notes: Card titles use large bold italic font (Anton) matching Figma. Title size adapts based on character count (short titles like '90%' are larger, longer titles like 'Fostering a Sense of Belonging' are smaller). Body text is standard size.

spacing.notes: Grid gap between cards is consistent. Section padding (py-10/sm:py-16) provides appropriate vertical spacing. Card internal padding (p-8) matches Figma's card content insets.

colors.notes: Cards use theme accent color (bg-accent via pseudo-element). Implementation uses purple accent; Figma shows cyan. This is a theme color variation, not a discrepancy.

borderRadius.notes: Cards have rounded-3xl (24px) corners matching the Figma design's rounded corners. Image cards have overflow-hidden to clip images to rounded corners.

images.notes: Image cards display with object-cover and proper focal point support. Images fill their cards completely with no distortion. Tall image cards span correctly.

contentResilience.notes: Test page includes stress tests: extremely long heading (section 5), single card (section 9), 8 cards in 4-column masonry (section 8), image-only grid (section 7). All handle gracefully without overflow or layout breakage.

notes.description: All ARIA changes are non-visual. The role='list' and role='listitem' attributes do not affect visual rendering. The alt text attribute on ImageCard has no visual impact (it only affects screen reader behavior). No layout regressions were introduced by these changes.

variationsCompared: Card #=4 long sentence (8 cards with longer text)
figmaComparison.overallMatch: Layout, spacing, typography, border radius, and card proportions match the Figma design across all variations. Accent color differs (theme variation, not a defect).
